business_description.nature_of_business: General contracting firm for commercial and institutional construction, including healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania; headquartered in Pittsburgh.

coverages_discussed.notes: Coverage requested for approximately 425 employees across all operations.
Occurrence form including standard premises/operations and products/completed operations.
Coverage for 15 vehicles including hired and non-owned auto.
Coverage for all scheduled locations on a special form with replacement cost valuation.

document_info.prepared_by: Patricia Nowak, Senior Broker, Steel City Insurance Group

extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The claims history warrants review; management has implemented corrective measures to address identified exposures. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 52 total claims with 1315756.27 in total paid losses and 1489697.06 total incurred. Currently, 12 claims remain open and 40 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% (avg annual incurred 297939.41 against current annual premium 1589164.70 over 5 loss years). Breakdown by coverage line: Workers' Compensation: 35 claims, 1126509.19 paid, 1176100.41 incurred; General Liability: 8 claims, 105420.70 paid, 176946.61 incurred; Commercial Auto: 8 claims, 47076.38 paid, 99900.04 incurred; Commercial Property: 1 claim, 36750.00 paid, 36750.00 incurred. Note: account experienced a cancellation in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group. Reason: Carrier non-renewed — book of business exit.
extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a general contracting firm for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh, with approximately 42 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers' Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: The insured is a general contracting firm for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh, with approximately 42 years of operating history.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. Management has invested significantly in employee safety training, with all field personnel OSHA-certified. The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually. The organization has achieved ISO 9001 certification, reflecting a commitment to quality and process control. The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements. Background checks and MVR reviews are conducted annually for all employees in safety-sensitive positions. With 425 employees and a structured HR program, the insured maintains low turnover and consistent safety training.

concerns: Claims history warrants review: 52 total claims over the review period, with 12 open and 40 closed.
Account experienced a cancellation/non-renewal in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group due to carrier book-of-business exit.
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: 52 total claims with 1315756.27 total paid losses and 1489697.06 total incurred; 12 claims remain open and 40 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% with average annual incurred of 297939.41 against current annual premium of 1589164.70 over 5 loss years.
positives: Formal return-to-work program reduced lost-time claims by over 30%.
Dedicated safety committee meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits.
All field personnel are OSHA-certified and management has invested significantly in safety training.
Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan is tested annually.
ISO 9001 certification reflects quality and process control commitment.
Contractual risk transfer provisions are included in all subcontractor and vendor agreements.
Background checks and MVR reviews are conducted annually for employees in safety-sensitive positions.
425 employees and structured HR program support low turnover and consistent safety training.
42 years of continuous operations under consistent management demonstrates operational stability.
Broker recommendation describes the account as a quality account with strong operational controls.
